import asyncio
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from typing import Optional, cast

from app.agents.core.graph_builder.checkpointer_manager import (
    get_checkpointer_manager,
)
from app.agents.core.nodes import (
    follow_up_actions_node,
    manage_system_prompts_node,
)
from app.agents.core.nodes.filter_messages import filter_messages_node
from app.agents.core.subagents.handoff_tools import handoff as handoff_tool
from app.agents.core.subagents.provider_subagents import register_subagent_providers
from app.agents.llm.client import init_llm
from app.agents.middleware import create_comms_middleware, create_executor_middleware
from app.agents.middleware.subagent import SubagentMiddleware
from app.agents.tools import memory_tools
from app.agents.tools.core.registry import get_tool_registry
from app.agents.tools.core.retrieval import get_retrieve_tools_function
from app.agents.tools.core.store import get_tools_store
from app.agents.tools.core.tool_runtime_config import (
    build_executor_child_tool_runtime_config,
)
from app.agents.tools.executor_tool import call_executor
from app.agents.tools.todo_tools import create_todo_pre_model_hook, create_todo_tools
from shared.py.wide_events import log
from app.core.lazy_loader import MissingKeyStrategy, lazy_provider
from app.override.langgraph_bigtool.create_agent import create_agent
from app.override.langgraph_bigtool.hooks import HookType
from langchain_core.language_models import LanguageModelLike
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import InMemorySaver


@asynccontextmanager
async def build_executor_graph(
    chat_llm: Optional[LanguageModelLike] = None,
    in_memory_checkpointer: bool = False,
):
    """Construct and compile the executor agent graph with handoff tools."""
    if chat_llm is None:
        chat_llm = init_llm()

    tool_registry, store = await asyncio.gather(
        get_tool_registry(),
        get_tools_store(),
    )

    todo_tools = create_todo_tools(source="executor")

    tool_dict = tool_registry.get_tool_dict()
    tool_dict.update({"handoff": handoff_tool})
    tool_dict.update({t.name: t for t in todo_tools})

    todo_hook = create_todo_pre_model_hook(source="executor")

    # Build excluded tool names for spawn_subagent: handoff and all subagent:-prefixed
    excluded_subagent_tools = {"handoff"}

    middleware = create_executor_middleware(
        subagent_excluded_tools=excluded_subagent_tools,
        subagent_tool_runtime_config=build_executor_child_tool_runtime_config(),
    )

    # Wire SubagentMiddleware with LLM and full tool registry
    subagent_mw = next(
        (mw for mw in middleware if isinstance(mw, SubagentMiddleware)),
        None,
    )
    if subagent_mw is None:
        log.warning(
            "SubagentMiddleware not found in middleware stack; spawn_subagent will be unavailable"
        )
    else:
        subagent_mw.set_llm(chat_llm)
        subagent_mw.set_tools(registry=tool_dict)
        subagent_mw.set_store(store)

    pre_model_hooks: list[HookType] = [
        cast(HookType, filter_messages_node),
        manage_system_prompts_node,
        todo_hook,
    ]

    builder = create_agent(
        llm=chat_llm,
        agent_name="executor_agent",
        tool_registry=tool_dict,
        retrieve_tools_coroutine=get_retrieve_tools_function(),
        initial_tool_ids=[
            "handoff",
            "plan_tasks",
            "update_tasks",
            "vfs_read",
            "vfs_cmd",
            "deep_research",
        ],
        middleware=middleware,
        pre_model_hooks=pre_model_hooks,
    )

    checkpointer_manager = await get_checkpointer_manager()

    model_name = getattr(chat_llm, "model_name", None) or getattr(
        chat_llm, "model", None
    )

    if in_memory_checkpointer or not checkpointer_manager:
        in_memory_checkpointer_instance = InMemorySaver()
        graph = builder.compile(
            checkpointer=in_memory_checkpointer_instance, store=store
        )
        log.debug("Graph compiled with in-memory checkpointer")
        log.set(agent={"model": model_name})
        yield graph
    else:
        postgres_checkpointer = checkpointer_manager.get_checkpointer()
        graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=postgres_checkpointer, store=store)
        log.debug("Graph compiled with PostgreSQL checkpointer")
        log.set(agent={"model": model_name})
        yield graph


@lazy_provider(
    name="executor_agent",
    required_keys=[],
    strategy=MissingKeyStrategy.WARN,
    auto_initialize=False,
)
async def build_executor_agent():
    """Build and return the executor agent with full tool access."""
    log.debug("Building executor agent with lazy providers")

    async with build_executor_graph() as graph:
        log.info("Executor agent built successfully")
    return graph


@asynccontextmanager
async def build_comms_graph(
    chat_llm: Optional[LanguageModelLike] = None,
    in_memory_checkpointer: bool = False,
):
    """Build the comms agent graph with only the executor tool."""
    if chat_llm is None:
        chat_llm = init_llm()

    tool_registry = {
        "call_executor": call_executor,
        "add_memory": memory_tools.add_memory,
        "search_memory": memory_tools.search_memory,
    }
    store = await get_tools_store()

    middleware = create_comms_middleware()

    pre_model_hooks: list[HookType] = [
        cast(HookType, filter_messages_node),
        manage_system_prompts_node,
    ]

    builder = create_agent(
        llm=chat_llm,
        agent_name="comms_agent",
        tool_registry=tool_registry,
        disable_retrieve_tools=True,
        initial_tool_ids=["call_executor", "add_memory", "search_memory"],
        middleware=middleware,
        pre_model_hooks=pre_model_hooks,
        end_graph_hooks=[
            follow_up_actions_node,
        ],
    )

    checkpointer_manager = await get_checkpointer_manager()

    model_name = getattr(chat_llm, "model_name", None) or getattr(
        chat_llm, "model", None
    )

    if in_memory_checkpointer or not checkpointer_manager:
        in_memory_checkpointer_instance = InMemorySaver()
        graph = builder.compile(
            checkpointer=in_memory_checkpointer_instance, store=store
        )
        log.debug("Comms graph compiled with in-memory checkpointer")
        log.set(agent={"model": model_name})
        yield graph
    else:
        postgres_checkpointer = checkpointer_manager.get_checkpointer()
        graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=postgres_checkpointer, store=store)
        log.debug("Comms graph compiled with PostgreSQL checkpointer")
        log.set(agent={"model": model_name})
        yield graph


@lazy_provider(
    name="comms_agent",
    required_keys=[],
    strategy=MissingKeyStrategy.WARN,
    auto_initialize=False,
)
async def build_comms_agent():
    """Build and return the comms agent using lazy providers."""
    log.debug("Building comms agent with lazy providers")

    async with build_comms_graph() as graph:
        log.info("Comms agent built successfully")
    return graph


def build_graphs():
    """Build comms and executor agents and register subagent providers."""
    log.info("Building core agent graphs...")

    register_subagent_providers()
    build_executor_agent()
    build_comms_agent()

    log.info("Core agent graphs built and registered successfully")

aiTitle: Fix WhatsApp bot message truncation and comms prompt

input.command: grep -l "early 20s\|human female friend\|WhatsApp Mode\|TONE MIRRORING" /tmp/gaia-api.log 2>&1 | head -3 && echo "---" && grep -c "You are GAIA" /tmp/gaia-api.log 2>&1 && echo "---" && grep -c "early 20s" /tmp/gaia-api.log 2>&1
input.description: Grep API log for comms prompt markers

input.command: grep -c "construct_langchain_messages\|create_system_message\|comms_agent\|history_message_count" /tmp/gaia-api.log 2>&1 && echo "---history counts---" && grep "history_message_count" /tmp/gaia-api.log 2>&1 | tail -10
grep -A1 "comms_agent\|chat_stream" /tmp/gaia-api.log 2>&1 | head -30

aiTitle: Fix WhatsApp bot message truncation and comms prompt

content.subject: Investigate truncation + asterisks via WA Web
content.description: Use Chrome DevTools to inspect the Kapso Sandbox conversation, find what was actually delivered (truncation marker, stray asterisks), and identify root causes.

content.subject: Fix markdown-span splitting in chunkResponse
content.description: Make chunkResponse refuse cuts that would split a `**bold**`, `*emph*`, `_X_`, `~X~`, or `` `code` `` span. Also avoid splitting `**bold**` patterns into stray asterisks.

content.subject: Verify comms prompt is actually in LLM input
content.description: LangSmith inputs only show memory SystemMessage, not the COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT. Confirm whether the system prompt is bound via create_react_agent's prompt= arg (separate path) or is genuinely missing from invocations.

content.subject: Convert markdown bullets to •
content.description: Update convertToWhatsAppMarkdown so `* item` and `- item` line starts become `• item`, since WhatsApp doesn't render markdown lists.

def build_initial_state(
    request,
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    history,
    trigger_context: Optional[dict] = None,
) -> dict:
    """Construct initial state dictionary for LangGraph execution.

    Builds the starting state containing all necessary context for the agent
    including user query, conversation history, tool selections, and optional
    workflow trigger context.

    Args:
        request: Message request object containing user message and selections
        user_id: Unique identifier for the user
        conversation_id: Unique identifier for the conversation thread
        history: List of previous messages in LangChain format
        trigger_context: Optional context data from workflow triggers

    Returns:
        State dictionary with query, messages, datetime, user context, and
        selected tools/workflows for graph processing
    """
    state = {
        "query": request.message,
        "intent": request.message,
        "messages": history,
        "current_datetime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "mem0_user_id": user_id,
        "conversation_id": conversation_id,
        "integration_usernames": {},
        "selected_tool": request.selectedTool,
        "selected_workflow": request.selectedWorkflow,
        "selected_calendar_event": request.selectedCalendarEvent,
    }

    if trigger_context:
        state["trigger_context"] = trigger_context

    return state

"""Pull the full inputs from the latest comms_agent LLM run and grep for COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT signature."""

import os
import sys
import json
from pathlib import Path

# Load LANGSMITH_API_KEY from primary .env
env_path = Path("/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia/apps/api/.env")
for line in env_path.read_text().splitlines():
    if "=" in line and not line.startswith("#"):
        k, _, v = line.partition("=")
        v = v.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
        if k and v:
            os.environ.setdefault(k, v)

from langsmith import Client

client = Client()

# Find the most recent run that's a comms_agent agent-node LLM call
runs = list(
    client.list_runs(
        project_name="default",
        run_type="llm",
        filter='and(eq(metadata_key,"langgraph_node"),eq(metadata_value,"agent"),eq(metadata_key,"agent_name"),eq(metadata_value,"comms_agent"))',
        limit=3,
    )
)
if not runs:
    print("No matching runs found")
    sys.exit(1)

for r in runs:
    print(f"\n=== Run {r.id} | start={r.start_time} | source={r.metadata.get('conversation_source')} ===")
    msgs = r.inputs.get("messages", [[]])[0]
    print(f"Total messages: {len(msgs)}")
    for i, m in enumerate(msgs):
        kw = m.get("kwargs", {})
        content = kw.get("content", "")
        is_memory = kw.get("additional_kwargs", {}).get("memory_message", False)
        msg_type = m.get("id", [""])[-1]
        head = content[:120].replace("\n", " ") if isinstance(content, str) else "<list>"
        print(f"  [{i:02d}] {msg_type:20s} memory={is_memory!s:5s} len={len(str(content)):6d} | {head}")

    # Aggregate all SystemMessage content (memory + non-memory) and search for comms prompt signature
    all_system = "\n---\n".join(
        m.get("kwargs", {}).get("content", "")
        for m in msgs
        if m.get("id", [""])[-1] == "SystemMessage"
        and isinstance(m.get("kwargs", {}).get("content"), str)
    )
    has_comms = "early 20s" in all_system or "human female friend" in all_system or "TONE MIRRORING" in all_system
    print(f"\n  COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT present in any SystemMessage? {has_comms}")

file.content:
creation via set_llm()/set_tools() since they aren't available at factory time.

    Args:
        subagent_llm: LLM for subagent execution
        subagent_tools: Tools available to subagents
        subagent_registry: Alternative tool registry for subagents
        subagent_excluded_tools: Tool names to exclude from subagent access
                                 (e.g., handoff, subagent:-prefixed tools)

    Returns:
        List of middleware for executor agent
    """
    return create_middleware_stack(
        enable_subagent=True,
        subagent_llm=subagent_llm,
        subagent_tools=subagent_tools,
        subagent_registry=subagent_registry,
        subagent_excluded_tools=subagent_excluded_tools,
        subagent_tool_runtime_config=subagent_tool_runtime_config,
        REDACTED | SPAWN_SUBAGENT_TOOL,
    )


def create_comms_middleware() -> list[Any]:
    """
    Create middleware stack for the comms agent.

    The comms agent handles user communication and delegates complex work
    to the executor. Only includes summarization and compaction middleware.

    Returns:
        List of middleware for comms agent
    """
    return create_middleware_stack(
        enable_subagent=False,
        REDACTED,
    )


def create_subagent_middleware(
    *,
    todo_source: str = "subagent",
    subagent_llm: LanguageModelLike | None = None,
    subagent_tools: list[BaseTool] | None = None,
    subagent_registry: Mapping[str, BaseTool] | None = None,
    subagent_excluded_tools: set[str] | None = None,
    subagent_tool_space: str = "general",
    subagent_tool_runtime_config: ToolRuntimeConfig | None = None,
) -> list[Any]:
    """
    Create middleware stack for provider subagents.

    Provider subagents handle focused integration work and should have:
    - SubagentMiddleware: For spawning focused sub-subagents
    - VFSCompactionMiddleware: Persist oversized tool outputs to VFS
    - NO summarization

    Spawned sub-subagents will NOT have SubagentMiddleware (enforced by
    SubagentMiddleware itself which excludes spawn_subagent from child tools).

content.subject: Investigate truncation + asterisks via WA Web
content.description: Use Chrome DevTools to inspect the Kapso Sandbox conversation, find what was actually delivered (truncation marker, stray asterisks), and identify root causes.

content.subject: Fix markdown-span splitting in chunkResponse
content.description: Make chunkResponse refuse cuts that would split a `**bold**`, `*emph*`, `_X_`, `~X~`, or `` `code` `` span. Also avoid splitting `**bold**` patterns into stray asterisks.

content.subject: Verify comms prompt is actually in LLM input
content.description: LangSmith inputs only show memory SystemMessage, not the COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT. Confirm whether the system prompt is bound via create_react_agent's prompt= arg (separate path) or is genuinely missing from invocations.

content.subject: Convert markdown bullets to •
content.description: Update convertToWhatsAppMarkdown so `* item` and `- item` line starts become `• item`, since WhatsApp doesn't render markdown lists.

file.content:
"""
Filter Messages Node for the conversational graph.

This module provides functionality to remove unanswered tool calls from AI messages
while preserving all other message types in their original order.
"""

from typing import TypeVar

from shared.py.wide_events import log
from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, AnyMessage, ToolMessage
from langchain_core.runnables import RunnableConfig
from langgraph.graph import MessagesState
from langgraph.store.base import BaseStore

T = TypeVar("T", bound=MessagesState)


def filter_messages_node(state: T, config: RunnableConfig, store: BaseStore) -> T:
    """
    Filters out unanswered tool calls from AI messages.

    This node scans the message history to identify tool calls that have
    corresponding ToolMessage responses. For AI messages with tool calls,
    only the tool calls that have responses are kept. This ensures incomplete
    tool interactions are cleaned up from the message history.

    Args:
        state: The current state containing messages.
        config: Configuration for the runnable.
        store: The store for any required data persistence.

    Returns:
        The updated state with unanswered tool calls removed from AI messages.
    """
    try:
        # First pass: collect all tool call IDs that have corresponding ToolMessage responses
        answered_tool_call_ids = set()

        for msg in state["messages"]:
            if isinstance(msg, ToolMessage):
                answered_tool_call_ids.add(msg.tool_call_id)

        # Second pass: filter messages
        filtered_messages: list[AnyMessage] = []

        for msg in state["messages"]:
            # For AI messages with tool calls, filter out unanswered tool calls
            if isinstance(msg, AIMessage) and msg.tool_calls:
                # Filter tool_calls to only include those with responses
                answered_tool_calls = [
                    tc
                    for tc in msg.tool_calls
                    if tc.get("id") in answered_tool_call_ids
                ]

                # Create a new AI message with filtered tool calls
                # We need to preserve the message even if all tool calls are filtered out
                # because it might contain important content/reasoning
                filtered_msg = msg.model_copy()
                filtered_msg.tool_calls = answered_tool_calls
                filtered_messages.append(filtered_msg)
            else:
                # Keep all other messages as-is (SystemMessage, HumanMessage, ToolMessage, etc.)
                filtered_messages.append(msg)

        return {**state, "messages": filtered_messages}  # type: ignore[return-value]

    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"Error in filter messages node: {e}")
        return state

tools_to_bind: list[BaseTool] = []
        if retrieve_tools is not None:
            tools_to_bind.append(retrieve_tools)
        tools_to_bind.extend(selected_tools)
        tools_to_bind.extend(initial_tools)
        tools_to_bind.extend(middleware_tools)
        tools_to_bind = dedupe_tool_bindings(tools_to_bind)
        llm_with_tools = _llm.bind_tools(tools_to_bind)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        response = llm_with_tools.invoke(state["messages"])

        if not response.tool_calls and not response.content:
            response.content = "Empty response from model."

        if isinstance(response.content, str) and agent_name == "comms_agent":
            response.content = response.content + NEW_MESSAGE_BREAKER

        return {"messages": [response]}  # type: ignore[return-value]

    async def acall_model(
        state: State, config: RunnableConfig, *, store: BaseStore
    ) -> State:
        """Async model invocation with middleware support."""
        state = await execute_hooks(pre_model_hooks, state, config, store)

        if middleware_executor:
            state = await middleware_executor.execute_before_model(state, config, store)

        model_configurations = config.get("configurable", {})
        _llm = llm.with_config(configurable=model_configurations)

        selected_tools = [tool_registry[id] for id in state["selected_tool_ids"]]
        initial_tools = [tool_registry[id] for id in (initial_tool_ids or [])]
        tools_to_bind: list[BaseTool] = []
        if retrieve_tools is not None:
            tools_to_bind.append(retrieve_tools)
        tools_to_bind.extend(selected_tools)
        tools_to_bind.extend(initial_tools)
        tools_to_bind.extend(middleware_tools)
        tools_to_bind = dedupe_tool_bindings(tools_to_bind)
        llm_with_tools = _llm.bind_tools(tools_to_bind)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

        try:
            recent_messages = state.get("messages", [])[-6:]
            preview = []
            for msg in recent_messages:
                role = msg.__class__.__name__
                content = getattr(msg, "content", "")
                if isinstance(content, str) and len(content) > 200:
                    content = content[:197] + "..."
                preview.append({"role": role, "content": content})
            log.info("acall_model message preview", preview=preview)
        except Exception as e:
            log.debug(f"Failed to log message preview: {e}")

        if middleware_executor and middleware_executor.has_wrap_model_call():
            middleware_tools_for_request: list[BaseTool | dict[str, Any]] = [
                tool for tool in tools_to_bind
            ]
            response = await middleware_executor.wrap_model_invocation(
                model=_llm,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
                state=state,
                config=config,
                store=store,
                tools=middleware_tools_for_request,
                invoke_fn=llm_with_tools.ainvoke,
            )
        else:
            response = await llm_with_tools.ainvoke(state["messages"])

        if not response.tool_calls and not response.content:
            response.content = "Empty response from model."

        if isinstance(response.content, str) and agent_name == "comms_agent":
            response.content = response.content + NEW_MESSAGE_BREAKER

        # Build updated state with response for after_model hooks
        updated_state: State = dict(state)  # type: ignore[assignment]
        updated_state["messages"] = list(state.get("messages", [])) + [response]

        # Execute middleware after_model hooks
        if middleware_executor:
            updated_state = await middleware_executor.execute_after_model(
                updated_state, config, store
            )

185	def get_platform_context_message(
186	    source: Optional[str] = None,
187	) -> Optional[SystemMessage]:
188	    """Create a system message informing the agent about the user's current platform.
189	
190	    This tells the LLM what output capabilities are available so it can adapt
191	    its formatting. Text-only platforms (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack)
192	    cannot render HTML, interactive UI components, or artifacts.
193	
194	    Args:
195	        source: The conversation source/platform identifier (e.g., "whatsapp", "web")
196	
197	    Returns:
198	        SystemMessage with platform context, or None if source is web/unset
199	    """
200	    if not source or source in ("web", "mobile"):
201	        return None
202	
203	    platform_info = {
204	        "whatsapp": {
205	            "name": "WhatsApp",
206	            "formatting": "WhatsApp formatting: *bold*, _italic_, ~strikethrough~, ```code```",
207	        },
208	        "telegram": {
209	            "name": "Telegram",
210	            "formatting": "Telegram formatting: **bold**, _italic_, `code`, ```code blocks```",
211	        },
212	        "discord": {
213	            "name": "Discord",
214	            "formatting": "Discord formatting: **bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~, `code`, ```code blocks```, > quotes",
215	        },
216	        "slack": {
217	            "name": "Slack",
218	            "formatting": "Slack formatting: *bold*, _italic_, ~strikethrough~, `code`, ```code blocks```, > quotes",
219	        },
220	    }
221	
222	    info = platform_info.get(source)
223	    if not info:
224	        return None
225	
226	    content = f"""—Platform Context (IMPORTANT)—
227	The user is messaging from **{info["name"]}**. This is a text-based messaging platform.
228	
229	OUTPUT RESTRICTIONS for this platform:
230	- NO HTML, interactive UI components, artifacts, or rich cards — the user cannot see them
231	- NO markdown links [text](url) — just paste URLs directly
232	- NO tables — use simple lists instead
233	- NO images or embedded media in your response
234	- Keep formatting simple and compatible: {info["formatting"]}
235	- The user CANNOT see tool_data UI, MCP apps, or any frontend components
236	- When showing structured data (search results, calendar events, emails, etc.), format as clean text lists
237	- Artifacts and HTML content blocks are invisible to the user — describe results in plain text instead
238	
239	WHAT TO DO INSTEAD:
240	- Present all information as formatted text using the platform's native formatting
241	- For data that would normally show as a card/component, write it out as a clear text summary
242	- For content that would be an artifact, include it directly in your message as text
243	- Keep messages concise — messaging platforms work best with shorter, focused messages"""
244	
245	    return SystemMessage(content=content)

aiTitle: Fix WhatsApp bot message truncation and comms prompt

content.text:
**Confirmed: `manage_system_prompts_node` is silently deleting `COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT` because `platform_msg` is treated as the "latest non-memory" system message.** Order in the message list each turn:

1. `COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT` (no flag)
2. `memory_msg` (memory_message=True)
3. `platform_msg` (no flag) ← **kept**
4. `HumanMessage`

The hook keeps only the latest non-memory system message → keeps platform context, drops the actual prompt.

Fix: mark platform_msg with `memory_message=True` so it's preserved alongside memory, leaving COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT as the legitimate "latest non-memory" survivor.

input.command:
cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-fix-whatsapp-truncation-comms-prompt && git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(bots): preserve comms prompt; markdown-aware chunking; bullet conversion

GAIA-633

Two live bugs surfaced when actually testing against WhatsApp/Kapso
that the previous commits did not catch:

1. The comms agent system prompt was being silently dropped from every
   LLM call. construct_langchain_messages adds three system messages per
   turn (COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT, memory_msg, platform_msg). Only memory_msg
   was flagged as memory-style; manage_system_prompts_node then treated
   platform_msg as the "latest non-memory system prompt" and discarded
   COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT. The model never saw GAIA's voice prompt — it
   responded as a generic assistant.

   Fix: mark platform_msg with additional_kwargs={"memory_message": True}
   so it is preserved alongside memory messages, leaving COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT
   as the legitimate latest non-memory survivor. Also migrated the existing
   memory_message=True kwargs in get_memory_message to use additional_kwargs
   explicitly so the flag survives the langchain serializer round-trip.

2. chunkResponse was splitting markdown emphasis spans (e.g. cutting
   inside ``**ELK Stack**``) and producing visible stray asterisks like
   ``Stack**:`` and ``*Jaeger`` after WhatsApp markdown conversion. Two
   compounding causes: ``**`` inside fenced code blocks (Python
   ``**kwargs``) was poisoning the parity check in cutsInsideEmphasisSpan,
   and the fallback orphan-balance step also counted code-fence ``**``.
   Both now strip code-fence content before counting markers. Verified
   live against WhatsApp / Kapso Sandbox: long markdown docs (with
   bold headings, bullet lists, links, and Python code blocks) split
   cleanly across multiple bubbles, no stray asterisks, no
   "... (truncated)" suffix.

Also: convertToWhatsAppMarkdown now turns leading ``- ``, ``* ``, ``+ ``
into ``• `` so markdown bullets render as actual bullets instead of
literal asterisks at the start of each line.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -25

prev=""
while true; do
  s=$(gh pr checks 667 --json name,bucket 2>/dev/null)
  cur=$(echo "$s" | jq -r '.[] | "\(.name): \(.bucket)"' | sort)
  diff <(echo "$prev") <(echo "$cur") | grep -E '^>' | sed 's/^> //'
  prev="$cur"
  if echo "$s" | jq -e 'all(.bucket != "pending")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$s" | jq -e 'length > 0' >/dev/null 2>&1; then break; fi
  fi
  sleep 30
done

content.text:
Clean working tree. Now waiting for CI to finish on PR #667. Background watcher will fire when done.

**Summary of what's been verified live in the Kapso Sandbox:**

| Test | Result |
|---|---|
| Casual "hi" | "Hey! What's up? How's your day going?" — comms prompt voice ✓ |
| Long markdown doc with `**headings**`, bullets, links, and `**kwargs` in code | 2 bubbles (3902 + 2066), clean paragraph cut, no stray asterisks, no truncation marker ✓ |
| 6000+ char Python module (single fenced code block forced to span boundary) | 2 bubbles (4089 + 2063), code fence balanced (closed at end of bubble 1, reopened at start of bubble 2), no truncation ✓ |

**Root causes that were live in production:**
1. `manage_system_prompts_node` was treating `platform_msg` as the latest non-memory system prompt and discarding `COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT` every turn → fixed by marking platform_msg as memory-style.
2. `**kwargs` inside fenced code blocks was poisoning the parity counter in `cutsInsideEmphasisSpan`, masking real orphan `**` markers in narrative text → fixed by stripping fenced content before counting markers.

CI will announce completion via the background watcher.

content.subject: Investigate truncation + asterisks via WA Web
content.description: Use Chrome DevTools to inspect the Kapso Sandbox conversation, find what was actually delivered (truncation marker, stray asterisks), and identify root causes.

content.subject: Fix markdown-span splitting in chunkResponse
content.description: Make chunkResponse refuse cuts that would split a `**bold**`, `*emph*`, `_X_`, `~X~`, or `` `code` `` span. Also avoid splitting `**bold**` patterns into stray asterisks.

content.subject: Verify comms prompt is actually in LLM input
content.description: LangSmith inputs only show memory SystemMessage, not the COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT. Confirm whether the system prompt is bound via create_react_agent's prompt= arg (separate path) or is genuinely missing from invocations.

content.subject: Convert markdown bullets to •
content.description: Update convertToWhatsAppMarkdown so `* item` and `- item` line starts become `• item`, since WhatsApp doesn't render markdown lists.

content.subject: Restart API + bot, validate live
content.description: Kill old API+bot, start fresh, send a test message via WhatsApp web (only Kapso Sandbox), verify chunking, comms prompt, and bullet rendering.

def _strip_openui_section(prompt: str) -> str:
    """Remove the embedded OpenUI component-instructions block from ``prompt``.

    The block is delimited by ``_OPENUI_SECTION_START_MARKER`` and
    ``_OPENUI_SECTION_END_MARKER``. Returns ``prompt`` unchanged if either
    marker is missing (defensive — keeps the build safe if the prompt is
    edited and the markers move).
    """
    start = prompt.find(_OPENUI_SECTION_START_MARKER)
    if start == -1:
        return prompt
    end_marker_idx = prompt.find(_OPENUI_SECTION_END_MARKER, start)
    if end_marker_idx == -1:
        return prompt
    end_of_line = prompt.find("\n", end_marker_idx + len(_OPENUI_SECTION_END_MARKER))
    end = end_of_line + 1 if end_of_line != -1 else len(prompt)
    # Collapse the surrounding blank lines so the result still reads cleanly.
    return prompt[:start].rstrip() + "\n\n" + prompt[end:].lstrip()


_COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT_PLAIN = _strip_openui_section(COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT)


def get_comms_agent_prompt(source: str | None = None) -> str:
    """Build the comms agent prompt.

    OpenUI Lang produces rich interactive cards that only the web / mobile /
    desktop clients can render. Messaging platforms (WhatsApp, Telegram,
    Discord, Slack) and email receive the raw ``:::openui`` fences as literal
    text, which looks broken. For those sources we omit BOTH the embedded
    OpenUI component-instructions section and the appended OpenUI Lang
    reference so the model falls back to plain Markdown that the
    platform-specific adapter can then format. Leaving the embedded section
    in (the previous behavior) caused the model to emit ``:::openui`` fences
    on WhatsApp anyway, drowning out the comms voice and contradicting the
    per-platform context message.
    """
    if source is None or source in RICH_UI_SOURCES:
        return COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT + "\n" + OPENUI_INSTRUCTIONS
    return _COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT_PLAIN


EXECUTOR_AGENT_PROMPT = """
You are GAIA's Executor.

ROLE

input.new_string:
def _strip_openui_section(prompt: str) -> str:
    """Remove the embedded OpenUI component-instructions block from ``prompt``.

    The block is delimited by ``_OPENUI_SECTION_START_MARKER`` and
    ``_OPENUI_SECTION_END_MARKER``. If either marker is missing we log a
    loud warning and return ``prompt`` unchanged — silently re-introducing
    the bug (plain prompt still telling the model to emit ``:::openui``)
    would be far worse than logging a noisy startup warning that someone
    edited the prompt and forgot to keep the markers in sync.
    """
    from shared.py.wide_events import log

    start = prompt.find(_OPENUI_SECTION_START_MARKER)
    if start == -1:
        log.warning(
            "comms_prompts: OpenUI section start marker not found in "
            "COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT — plain (whatsapp/telegram/discord/slack) "
            "variant will still contain OpenUI instructions. Update "
            "_OPENUI_SECTION_START_MARKER to match the prompt."
        )
        return prompt
    end_marker_idx = prompt.find(_OPENUI_SECTION_END_MARKER, start)
    if end_marker_idx == -1:
        log.warning(
            "comms_prompts: OpenUI section end marker not found after the "
            "start marker — plain variant strip aborted. Update "
            "_OPENUI_SECTION_END_MARKER to match the prompt."
        )
        return prompt
    end_of_line = prompt.find("\n", end_marker_idx + len(_OPENUI_SECTION_END_MARKER))
    end = end_of_line + 1 if end_of_line != -1 else len(prompt)
    # Collapse the surrounding blank lines so the result still reads cleanly.
    return prompt[:start].rstrip() + "\n\n" + prompt[end:].lstrip()

cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-fix-whatsapp-truncation-comms-prompt && git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(bots): preserve code indentation across cuts; warn on missing OpenUI markers

Two follow-up fixes flagged by review:

- chunkResponse no longer trims whitespace when the cut lands inside a
  fenced code block. The previous unconditional trimEnd/trimStart pair
  would strip leading indentation from Python/YAML code that resumes in
  the next bubble, producing visually broken code. Trimming now only
  runs for narrative cuts; fenced cuts append/prepend the balancing
  markers verbatim so deep indentation survives.

- _strip_openui_section logs a loud warning when either OpenUI section
  marker is missing, instead of silently returning the prompt
  unchanged. A silent miss would silently re-introduce the original
  bug (plain WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord/Slack prompt still containing
  OpenUI instructions); the warning makes any future prompt edit that
  drifts from the markers immediately obvious at startup.

Verified live against Kapso Sandbox: a deeply indented multi-class
Python module (5500+ chars, single fenced block) splits cleanly across
3 bubbles with 40-space indentation preserved at the cut boundaries
and no truncation marker.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

content.text:
CI watcher is running in the background. While we wait, here's the current state:

**PR #667 (https://github.com/theexperiencecompany/gaia/pull/667)** → `develop`, references **GAIA-633**

**Live verified against Kapso Sandbox:**

| Test | Result |
|---|---|
| Casual "hi" | "Hey! What's up? How's your day going?" — GAIA voice ✓ |
| Long markdown doc with `**headings**`, bullets, links, `**kwargs` in code | 2 bubbles, clean cut at paragraph boundary, no stray asterisks, no `(truncated)` ✓ |
| 6000+ char Python module spanning a fence | 2 bubbles, fence closed/reopened, code blocks render correctly ✓ |
| 5500+ char deeply indented Python class | 3 bubbles, **40-space indentation preserved across cut boundaries** ✓ |

**Root causes fixed (commits in the branch):**
1. `manage_system_prompts_node` was discarding `COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT` because `platform_msg` masqueraded as the latest agent prompt → fixed by marking platform_msg as memory-style.
2. `**kwargs` inside fenced code blocks was poisoning the parity check in `cutsInsideEmphasisSpan` → fixed by stripping fenced content before counting markers.
3. `trimStart()` was stripping significant code indentation when cuts landed inside a fenced block → trimming now only runs for narrative cuts.
4. `_strip_openui_section` silently no-op'd if markers drifted → now logs a loud warning so the regression is immediately visible at startup.

Background watcher will fire when CI finishes the second push.

content.text:
Done. Branch is fully pushed, all required checks pass.

## Final state

**PR #667** → `develop`, references **GAIA-633**, status: **MERGEABLE**

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| **quality-gate** (only required check) | ✅ SUCCESS |
| build, dead-code, detect, static-python, static-typescript, test-python, test-typescript, trivy-scan, changelog-sync, Validate PR Title, Vercel Preview Comments | ✅ SUCCESS |
| coverage, Mintlify Deployment, trigger-build | ⏭️ SKIPPED (no relevant changes) |
| Vercel Agent Review | NEUTRAL (advisory) |
| SonarCloud Code Analysis | FAILURE (advisory — not a required check; flags 1 security hotspot but quality-gate is the only required gate) |

**Mergeable**: yes. The branch is `BEHIND` develop and will need a rebase before merge, but I'm not merging it (your absolute rule).

## What was actually broken in production

1. **Comms prompt was being silently dropped from every WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord/Slack LLM call.** `manage_system_prompts_node` treated the platform context message as the "latest non-memory system prompt" and discarded the actual `COMMS_AGENT_PROMPT`. The model never saw GAIA's voice prompt.
2. **`... (truncated)` appended to long replies.** Old `truncateResponse` was clamping output instead of chunking.
3. **Stray `*` and `**` in narrative text.** chunkResponse was splitting `**bold**` mid-span; `**kwargs` inside Python code was poisoning the parity check.
4. **Markdown bullets rendered as literal `*`.** WhatsApp doesn't render markdown lists.
5. **Code-block indentation lost** when cut landed inside a fenced block (`trimStart` ate Python's leading spaces).

All five fixed and verified live in the Kapso Sandbox. Loading state (typing indicator) confirmed working throughout.

input.subject: Protect single-asterisk WhatsApp bold (`*X*`) from cuts
input.description: Extend cutsInsideEmphasisSpan and the orphan-balance step to handle single-asterisk emphasis (the WhatsApp-native bold marker). The platform context tells the model to use `*bold*` so the LLM emits single-asterisk pairs that can be split by chunkResponse, leaving orphan asterisks in narrative.

content.content: Task #13 created successfully: Protect single-asterisk WhatsApp bold (`*X*`) from cuts

task.subject: Protect single-asterisk WhatsApp bold (`*X*`) from cuts

content.content: Updated task #13 status
The file /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-fix-whatsapp-truncation-comms-prompt/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/index.ts has been updated successfully.

lines: + * Strips fenced code blocks (``` ... ```) so the parity checks only see
+ * narrative text. Code can legitimately contain ``**`` (Python ``**kwargs``),
+ * lone ``*`` (shell globs ``*.txt``), or stray backticks, and those bytes
+ * must not poison emphasis parity for prose.
+ * Counts single ``*`` markers in ``text``, excluding any ``*`` that is part
+ * of a ``**`` (markdown bold). Used to detect WhatsApp-native ``*bold*``
+ * pairs that the model emits when the platform context tells it to use
+ * single-asterisk bold. Markdown bullets at line start (``^* ``) are also
+ * excluded — they are not paired emphasis markers.
+  // Drop ``**`` first so the bold pairs don't double-count toward single-`*`.
+  // Drop bullet-list ``*`` followed by a space at line start.
* Returns true if cutting ``text`` at index ``idx`` would land inside an
- * unclosed markdown emphasis span. We only check the two markers that bite us
- * in practice: ``**bold**`` (model emits markdown bold even when output goes
- * to WhatsApp) and `` `inline code` ``. Single ``*``, ``_``, ``~`` are skipped
- * because they appear in code identifiers, URLs, and plain prose — the false
- * positive rate is too high to be worth the rare valid catch.
+ * unclosed markdown emphasis span. We check the markers that actually bite us
+ * WhatsApp-native bold the platform context steers the model toward), and
+ * `` `inline code` ``. ``_`` and ``~`` are skipped because they appear in
+ * identifiers/URLs and the false-positive rate would shrink chunks
- * The check is symmetric: an odd number of ``**`` in the prefix AND a closing
- * ``**`` somewhere in the suffix means the cut would orphan one half of the
- * pair. Same logic for backticks (excluding triple-backticks, which are
- * handled separately by the fence-balancing step in {@link chunkResponse}).
+ * The check is symmetric: an odd number of a marker in the prefix AND a

cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-fix-whatsapp-truncation-comms-prompt && git diff --stat HEAD && echo "---"; git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(bots): protect single-asterisk WhatsApp bold from cuts

The platform context message tells the model to use ``*bold*`` (single
asterisk) on WhatsApp, so the LLM emits single-asterisk emphasis pairs
that the previous double-asterisk-only protection didn't cover. Cuts
landing inside ``*Section Heading*`` produced visible stray ``*`` glyphs
in the rendered bubbles even after the ``**X**`` work.

- cutsInsideEmphasisSpan now also rejects boundaries inside an unclosed
  single-asterisk pair, alongside the existing ``**X**`` and inline
  backtick checks.
- The post-cut orphan-balance step strips an orphan ``*`` from the
  chunk's narrative tail and the matching opener from the next chunk's
  head.
- Both ignore fenced code (``**kwargs``, ``*.txt`` globs) and bullet
  ``* `` at line start, since neither participates in emphasis pairing.
- stripFencedBlocks extracted to a top-level helper since it is now
  used by both the parity checks and the orphan-balance step.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8

content.content: Updated task #13 status

content.text:
## What was actually going on

The platform context message tells the model to use **`*bold*`** (single asterisk) on WhatsApp, so the LLM was emitting single-asterisk emphasis pairs like `*Section Heading*`. My previous protection only covered `**X**` (double asterisks) and inline backticks — it left single-asterisk bold completely unguarded. Any cut landing inside `*Section Heading*` orphaned one half of the pair, and you saw stray `*` glyphs in the bubbles.

## What's now in place (just pushed to PR #667)

1. `cutsInsideEmphasisSpan` rejects boundaries that would split an unclosed single-asterisk pair, in addition to `**X**` and inline backticks.
2. The post-cut orphan-balance step strips an orphan `*` from the chunk tail and the corresponding closing `*` from the next chunk's head — same pattern as the `**` handling.
3. Both passes ignore fenced code (so `**kwargs` and `*.txt` globs don't poison the parity) and ignore `* ` at line start (markdown bullet, not emphasis).

## Why I can't verify it myself right now

The WhatsApp Web tab in Chrome got closed (the DevTools MCP can only see `about:blank`), so I can't drive the conversation programmatically. Bot + API are still running locally, ngrok webhook is still pointed at `https://8605-2409-40c1-642b-f16f-69e6-ca41-8311-ba48.ngrok-free.app/webhook`, the new code is live (tsx watch reloaded after the restart). 

Please send the same prompt yourself: **`give me a long markdown reference doc with *bold headings*, several bullet lists, two fenced code blocks (python and bash with kwargs and globs), and inline links — at least 6500 chars`** — and let me know what you see. If any stray asterisks remain I'll re-add the chunkResponse debug log and chase the next root cause instead of assuming.

content.content:
100	 */
101	function applyOutsideCodeBlocks(
102	  text: string,
103	  transform: (segment: string) => string,
104	): string {
105	  const parts: string[] = [];
106	  let lastIndex = 0;
107	  for (const match of text.matchAll(/```[\s\S]*?```/g)) {
108	    parts.push(transform(text.slice(lastIndex, match.index)));
109	    parts.push(match[0]);
110	    lastIndex = (match.index ?? 0) + match[0].length;
111	  }
112	  parts.push(transform(text.slice(lastIndex)));
113	  return parts.join("");
114	}
115	
116	/**
117	 * Converts standard CommonMark Markdown to Telegram legacy Markdown.
118	 *
119	 * Telegram's legacy `Markdown` parse mode supports:
120	 * `*bold*`, `_italic_`, `` `code` ``, ` ```code``` `, `[text](url)`.
121	 *
122	 * Converts `**bold**` → `*bold*`, strips unsupported `# headers` to bold,
123	 * and removes blockquote `>` prefixes and horizontal rules.
124	 * Code blocks are preserved unchanged.
125	 */
126	export function convertToTelegramMarkdown(text: string): string {
127	  return applyOutsideCodeBlocks(
128	    text,
129	    (segment) =>
130	      segment
131	        .replace(/\*\*\*(.+?)\*\*\*/g, "*$1*") // ***bold italic*** → *bold*
132	        .replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, "*$1*") // **bold** → *bold*
133	        .replace(/^#{1,6}\s+(.+)$/gm, "*$1*") // # Heading → *Heading*
134	        .replace(/^>\s*/gm, "") // > quote → strip prefix
135	        .replace(/^[-_]{3,}$/gm, ""), // --- / ___ → remove
136	  );
137	}
138	
139	/**
140	 * Converts standard CommonMark Markdown to Slack mrkdwn.
141	 *
142	 * Slack mrkdwn supports: `*bold*`, `_italic_`, `` `code` ``, ` ```code``` `,
143	 * `<url|label>` hyperlinks.
144	 *
145	 * Converts `**bold**` → `*bold*`, `[label](url)` → `<url|label>`,
146	 * strips `# headers` to bold, strips blockquote `>` prefixes and horizontal rules.
147	 * Code blocks are preserved unchanged.
148	 */
149	export function convertToSlackMrkdwn(text: string): string {
150	  return applyOutsideCodeBlocks(
151	    text,
152	    (segment) =>
153	      segment
154	        .replace(/\*\*\*(.+?)\*\*\*/g, "*$1*") // ***bold italic*** → *bold*
155	        .replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, "*$1*") // **bold** → *bold*
156	        .replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, "<$2|$1>") // [label](url) → <url|label>
157	        .replace(/^#{1,6}\s+(.+)$/gm, "*$1*") // # Heading → *Heading*
158	        .replace(/^>\s*/gm, "") // > quote → strip prefix
159	        .replace(/^[-_]{3,}$/gm, ""), // --- / ___ → remove
160	  );
161	}
162	
163	/**
164	 * Converts standard CommonMark Markdown to WhatsApp-compatible formatting.
165	 *
166	 * WhatsApp supports: `*bold*`, `_italic_`, `~strikethrough~`, `` `code` ``,
167	 * ` ```code``` `. Links are shown as bare URLs (WhatsApp auto-links them).
168	 *
169	 * Converts `**bold**` → `*bold*`, `[label](url)` → `label (url)`,
170	 * strips `# headers` to bold, strips blockquote `>` prefixes and horizontal rules.
171	 * Code blocks are preserved unchanged.
172	 */
173	export function convertToWhatsAppMarkdown(text: string): string {
174	  return applyOutsideCodeBlocks(
175	    text,
176	    (segment) =>
177	      segment
178	        .replaceAll(/\*\*\*([^*]+)\*\*\*/g, "*$1*") // ***bold italic*** → *bold*
179	        .replaceAll(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, "*$1*") // **bold** → *bold*
180	        .replaceAll(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, "$1 ($2)") // [label](url) → label (url)
181	        .replaceAll(/^#{1,6}\s+(.+)$/gm, "*$1*") // # Heading → *Heading*
182	        .replaceAll(/^[-_]{3,}$/gm, "") // --- / ___ → remove (BEFORE bullets)
183	        .replaceAll(/^(\s*)[*\-+]\s+/gm, "$1• ") // - / * / + bullet → •
184	        .replaceAll(/^>\s*/gm, ""), // > quote → strip prefix
185	  );
186	}

**5.2 Authentication**
We utilize OAuth 2.0 (https://oauth.net/2/) and OpenID Connect (https://openid.net/connect/) for all identity management.

**6. Monitoring and Observability**
We use the following tools to maintain system health:

• *Prometheus*: For time-series metrics collection.
• *Grafana*: For visualization and alerting.
• *ELK Stack*: For centralized log aggregation and analysis.
• *Jaeger*: For distributed tracing across microservices.

**7. Incident Management**
When an incident occurs, we follow a structured response process.

"The goal is to restore service as quickly as possible, then conduct a blameless post-mortem."

**7.1 Response Steps**
• *Detection*: Automated alerts trigger a PagerDuty incident.
• *Triage*: The on-call engineer assesses the severity.
• *Mitigation*: The team works to restore service.
• *Post-Mortem*: A document is created to analyze the root cause and prevent recurrence.

**8. Operational Excellence**
Maintaining high availability requires rigorous operational standards.

**8.1 Deployment Pipeline**
• *Commit*: Code is pushed to the repository.
• *Build*: Docker images are built and scanned for vulnerabilities.
• *Test*: Automated integration tests are executed in a staging environment.
• *Deploy*: Blue-Green deployment strategy is used to ensure zero downtime.

**9. Future Roadmap**
• *Q3 2026*: Migration to serverless architecture for non-critical services.
• *Q4 2026*: Implementation of AI-driven anomaly detection.

**10. Appendix**
**10.1 Acronyms**
• *API*: Application Programming Interface
• *CI/CD*: Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment
• *SRE*: Site Reliability Engineering
• *VPC*: Virtual Private Cloud

**10.2 External Resources**
• Official Python Documentation (https://docs.python.org)
• Terraform Registry (https://registry.terraform.io)
• Kubernetes Documentation (https://kubernetes.io/docs)
• Internal Engineering Wiki (https://wiki.internal.example.com)

**11. Conclusion**
This guide provides the foundation for our engineering practices. By following these standards, we ensure that our systems remain robust, scalable, and secure.

***

*End of Reference Guide*

/**
 * Pure display formatters for bot responses.
 *
 * These are data-in, string-out functions with no side effects.
 * They are used by the shared command handlers in commands.ts
 * and can also be called directly when assembling custom responses.
 *
 * formatBotError is the single error formatter for all bots.
 * It checks for GaiaApiError (preserves HTTP status), then
 * falls back to axios-style errors, then generic Error messages.
 */
import { GaiaApiError } from "../api";
/**
 * Formats a workflow for display in a bot message.
 */
export function formatWorkflow(workflow) {
    const status = workflow.status === "active"
        ? "✅"
        : workflow.status === "draft"
            ? "📝"
            : "⏸️";
    return `${status} **${workflow.name}**\nID: \`${workflow.id}\`

content.content:
export function convertToWhatsAppMarkdown(text) {
    return applyOutsideCodeBlocks(text, (segment) => segment
        .replaceAll(/\*\*\*([^*]+)\*\*\*/g, "*$1*") // ***bold italic*** → *bold*
        .replaceAll(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, "*$1*") // **bold** → *bold*
        .replaceAll(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, "$1 ($2)") // [label](url) → label (url)
        .replaceAll(/^#{1,6}\s+(.+)$/gm, "*$1*") // # Heading → *Heading*
        .replaceAll(/^>\s*/gm, "") // > quote → strip prefix
        .replaceAll(/^[-_]{3,}$/gm, ""));
}
/**
 * Formats authentication required message with clear onboarding steps.
 */
export function formatAuthRequiredMessage(platform, authUrl, context) {
    const platformName = platform.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + platform.slice(1);
    return (`🔐 **Authentication Required**\n\n` +
        `To ${context || "use this feature"}, link your ${platformName} account to GAIA.\n\n` +
        `**Steps:**\n` +
        `1. Click: ${authUrl}\n` +
        `2. Sign in to GAIA (or create account)\n` +
        `3. Confirm connection\n` +
        `4. Return and try again!\n\n` +

content.text:
**Found the bug.** The heading regex wraps captured content in `*X*` — but if the content already has `**X**` (from the model emitting `### **Heading**`), the bold rule has already converted it to `*Heading*` first, then the heading rule wraps that in `*` → `**Heading**` (back to double asterisks). Same problem if the model emits `### *Heading*` directly.

The fix: do heading conversion FIRST, then run the bold rules so `***X***` (the wrapped result) collapses to `*X*`. Also add `*` to the horizontal-rule remover.

lines: +        // Headings FIRST so the content gets wrapped in ``*`` before the
+        // bold rule sees it. Otherwise the model's ``### **Heading**`` would
+        // the heading rule wraps the already-emphasised content in ``*`` —
+        // re-introducing the double asterisks we tried to remove.

content.text:
**Found the actual bug.** The bold regex `/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g` is matching across blank lines — `***

**Heading**` matches the LAST 2 chars of `***` + `

` + the OPENING `**` of `**Heading**`, replacing them with `*

*` and leaving orphan `**` at the end of "Heading**". I need to restrict `[^*]` to also exclude newlines so bold can only span a single paragraph.

content.content: The file /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-fix-whatsapp-truncation-comms-prompt/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/formatters.ts has been updated successfully.

lines: // the heading rule wraps the already-emphasised content in ``*`` —
// re-introducing the double asterisks we tried to remove.
+        // Horizontal-rule remover MUST run before the bold rule. Otherwise
+        // ``***`` on its own line followed by ``**Heading**`` lets the bold
+        // regex's ``[^*]`` greedy-match the inter-line newlines and pair
+        // chars across the ``***`` boundary into ``**X**``, splitting the
+        // ``**Heading**`` and leaving stray ``**`` glyphs in the output.
+        // Bold rules: keep ``[^*\n]`` (no newlines) so a single ``**`` opener
+        // cannot reach across blank lines and accidentally pair with the
+        // opener of a SEPARATE bold span.

cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-fix-whatsapp-truncation-comms-prompt && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(bots): WhatsApp markdown converter cycled `**X**` back to `**X**`

Three compounding bugs in convertToWhatsAppMarkdown were cycling
``**Heading**`` (or ``### **Heading**``) right back to ``**Heading**``
in the final WhatsApp text, so users saw literal ``**`` glyphs in the
rendered bubbles even after the chunking fixes:

1. Heading rule ran AFTER the bold rule. Sequence:
   ``### **Heading**`` → ``### *Heading*`` (bold collapses)
                      → ``**Heading**``    (heading wraps in `*`)
   Net result: original ``**`` re-introduced. Heading now runs FIRST so
   the wrapped content goes through the bold rule afterwards and the
   resulting ``***Heading***`` collapses to ``*Heading*``.

2. Horizontal-rule remover only matched ``---`` / ``___``, not ``***``.
   So a markdown ``***`` separator survived.

3. The bold regex ``/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g`` matched ACROSS newlines. With
   text like ``***\n\n**Heading**...``, the last 2 chars of ``***``
   would pair with the OPENING ``**`` of the next bold span via the
   ``\n\n`` between them (no asterisks → matches ``[^*]+``). Result:
   ``*\n\n*Heading**...`` with a stray ``**`` orphan after Heading.
   Restricting the inner class to ``[^*\n]`` keeps each bold span
   confined to a single paragraph and stops the cross-paragraph pairing.

Verified live against Kapso Sandbox: ``### *5.2 Authentication*`` and
``**6. Monitoring and Observability**`` both arrive at sendWhatsAppText
as ``*5.2 Authentication*`` / ``*6. Monitoring and Observability*``,
no stray ``**`` in narrative, ``**kwargs`` and other code-fence
content untouched.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

content.text:
Pushed to PR #667. CI watch is already running on the prior push and will fire when complete.

## What was actually wrong (this round)

Three bugs in `convertToWhatsAppMarkdown` were cycling `**Heading**` straight back to `**Heading**`:

1. **Heading rule ran AFTER bold.** Sequence: `### **Heading**` → `### *Heading*` (bold strips one layer) → `**Heading**` (heading wraps it back in `*`). Same result whether the model emitted `### **X**` or `### *X*`. Fixed by running heading FIRST.

2. **Horizontal-rule remover skipped `***`.** It only matched `---` and `___`, so markdown `***` separators survived.

3. **Bold regex matched across newlines** (the actual nasty one). Text like `***\n\n**Heading**...` made `/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g` pair the last 2 asterisks of `***` with the OPENING `**` of `**Heading**` (the `\n\n` between them is non-asterisk so `[^*]+` happily matched). The replacement gave `*\n\n*Heading**` — one half of a different pair eaten, the other half left orphan. Restricted the inner class to `[^*\n]` so each bold span is confined to a single paragraph.

**Verified live:** `### *5.2 Authentication*` (model output) → `*5.2 Authentication*` at the wire, `**` count in outbound bubbles dropped to 0 outside code blocks (only `**kwargs` inside Python code remains, which is correct). Bullets render as `•`, links as `text (url)`, no stray asterisks in WhatsApp.

Debug logs removed from the adapter; tmp test files cleaned up.
